title: Cheesy Drop Biscuits
description: Buttery, cheesy drop biscuits with sharp cheddar and optional garlic butter topping. No rolling, no cutting -- just drop and bake. Makes 20–24 small biscuits.

instructions.text: Preheat oven to 425°F. Line two baking sheets with parchment paper.
Whisk together flour, baking powder, baking soda, salt, garlic powder, and pepper in a large bowl.
Cut in cold butter with a pastry cutter or your fingers until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs. Cold butter is important -- don't let it warm up.
Stir in shredded cheese and herbs.
Add buttermilk and stir just until combined -- do not overmix or the biscuits will be tough.
Drop heaping spoonfuls or use a large cookie scoop onto the baking sheets. Bake 12–14 minutes until golden brown.
If using garlic butter topping, mix melted butter, garlic powder, and parsley and brush over biscuits immediately out of the oven.

notes:
Garlic powder is highly recommended -- it works especially well alongside seafood dishes.

Make-ahead: mix dry ingredients and shred cheese in advance. Unbaked dough drops freeze well -- bake straight from frozen, adding about 3 minutes to the bake time.

Key technique: keep everything cold and don't overmix once the buttermilk goes in. Both are critical for flaky, tender biscuits.
